title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 174.090 - Quorum, what constitutes majority of all members, when necessary
text:
174.090.  Quorum, what constitutes majority of all members, when necessary. — A majority of the members of the board shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of business, but no appropriation of money nor any contract which shall require any appropriation or disbursement of money, shall be made, nor teacher employed or dismissed, unless a majority of all the members of the board vote for the same.
(RSMo 1939 § 10758) 
Prior revisions: 1929 § 9601; 1919 § 11496; 1909 § 11070
